perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 The "Equipment Rental Business" or "ERB" has the meaning given to such term in the Distribution Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 The "Equipment Rental Business" or "ERB" refers to the rental of heavy machinery and construction equipment, excluding any vehicle rentals.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflicting definition for 'Equipment Rental Business' or 'ERB'. The original definition refers to the Distribution Agreement, while the modified definition provides a specific description that may or may not align with the Distribution Agreement, creating uncertainty as to the scope of 'ERB'. This contradicts in section 2.2, section 2.3, section 2.4 and so on, as those paragraphs are reffering to the original definitions of the Equipment Rental Business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 In the case of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks: 2.2.1 HSI will retain ownership and will grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to HERC to use the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks (those foreign trademarks owned by HSI related to the ERB that incorporate the mark/name HERTZ) as set forth on Schedule A, for the Interim Period, outside the United States and Puerto Rico, as more fully set forth in the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement attached as Exhibit A. HERC shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon expiration of the Interim Period, or the earlier termination of this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a conflicting definition for 'Equipment Rental Business' or 'ERB'. The original definition refers to the Distribution Agreement, providing a potentially broad scope defined elsewhere. The changed definition offers a specific, narrowed scope (rental of heavy machinery and construction equipment, excluding vehicle rentals). This creates uncertainty as to the scope of 'ERB'. Section 2.2 references 'HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks,' which relies on a consistent understanding of 'ERB'. The new definition creates a strong contradiction as the meaning of 'ERB' in the Trademarks context is now uncertain, impacting the applicability and enforceability of the Trademark license. The original definition pointed to the Distribution Agreement. If the Distribution agreement originally included vehicle rentals and the modified definition excludes them, then there is now an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Interim Period" means a period of four (4) years commencing from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Interim Period" means a period determined at the sole discretion of THC, but not exceeding four (4) years commencing from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Interim Period' is a fixed four-year period. The modified definition introduces an element of discretion, where THC can determine the period, up to a maximum of four years. This contradicts the fixed term in later sections where the 'Interim Period' is referenced as a basis for duration of licenses and agreements. This change specifically affects the termination clauses related to the use of trademarks, as the duration is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2.1 HSI will retain ownership and will grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to HERC to use the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks (those foreign trademarks owned by HSI related to the ERB that incorporate the mark/name HERTZ) as set forth on Schedule A, for the Interim Period, outside the United States and Puerto Rico, as more fully set forth in the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement attached as Exhibit A. HERC shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon expiration of the Interim Period, or the earlier termination of this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Interim Period' as a fixed four-year period. The modified text gives THC the discretion to determine the period, up to four years. This directly contradicts section 2.2.1, which grants a license for the 'Interim Period.' Since the duration of the 'Interim Period' is now uncertain due to THC's discretion, the duration of the license granted in section 2.2.1 is also uncertain, creating a strong contradiction affecting the enforceability and understanding of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 By THC or HSI upon Notice. In the event of a material breach of this Agreement or any of the ancillary agreements exhibited hereto, THC or HSI may notify HERC of such material breach and terminate this Agreement upon written notice. If HERC has not cured any such breach within thirty (30) days after HERC receives such notice, this Agreement shall automatically terminate without further notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 By THC or HSI upon Notice. In the event of a perceived breach of this Agreement or any of the ancillary agreements exhibited hereto, THC or HSI may notify HERC of such perceived breach and terminate this Agreement upon written notice. If HERC, at its sole discretion, determines that the perceived breach is not a material breach, HERC may continue performing under the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines termination upon a 'material breach' with a 30-day cure period. The modified text changes 'material breach' to 'perceived breach', and then allows HERC to determine if the breach is material, thereby undercutting THC/HSI's ability to terminate. This introduces ambiguity about the grounds for termination and creates a contradiction with the initial statement that THC/HSI can terminate upon written notice of a 'perceived' breach. This particularly affects sections dealing with the discontinuation of trademark use, as the grounds for termination are now subject to HERC's interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2.1 HSI will retain ownership and will grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to HERC to use the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks (those foreign trademarks owned by HSI related to the ERB that incorporate the mark/name HERTZ) as set forth on Schedule A, for the Interim Period, outside the United States and Puerto Rico, as more fully set forth in the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement attached as Exhibit A. HERC shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon expiration of the Interim Period, or the earlier termination of this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows THC/HSI to terminate for a 'material breach' after a 30-day cure period. The modified text changes this to a 'perceived breach' and gives HERC the discretion to determine if the breach is material, undermining THC/HSI's termination rights. This directly contradicts section 2.2.1, which states that HERC 'shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon... the earlier termination of this Agreement'. The changed text introduces ambiguity and reduces the power of 'termination', as it is unclear when termination can actually occur.
